name: 生命历程与轨迹表达
description: 用于把事件、状态和结果写成随年龄、婚姻历程、队列或历史时间展开的动态过程。

clusters.meaning: 把事件、状态和结果写成轨迹与转换

expressions.note: 用于把状态序列写成过程，避免只描述一次性 outcome。
expressions.example: We compare occupational trajectories over the marital course.

expressions.note: 适合区分年龄变化与历史时期变化，不能简单等同于 age group。
expressions.example: Younger cohorts reported higher depressive symptoms.

expressions.note: 强调随时间恶化，适合 trajectory 或 cohort 变化。
expressions.example: The trend was driven by deteriorated mental health.

expressions.note: 比 different outcomes 更能表达时间过程和不平等累积。
expressions.example: The study documents diverging trajectories by education.

expressions.note: 把 timing、order、duration、number 放进一个过程框架。
expressions.example: We classify union transition trajectories from ages 16 to 30.

expressions.note: gendered 表示差异由性别制度和角色塑造，不只是男女分组。
expressions.example: Gendered depression trajectories vary across cohorts.

expressions.note: 比 breakup 更正式，适合和 stress、distress、life event 搭配。
expressions.example: Relationship dissolution can trigger psychological distress.

expressions.note: 强调 parenthood 与婚姻制度错位，适合写 life course overlap。
expressions.example: Dissolution intertwined with non-marital parenthood is consequential.

clusters.meaning: 生命历程阶段、婚姻历程和领域范围限定

expressions.note: 比 mental health outcomes 更动态，适合 growth curve 写法。
expressions.example: We estimate psychological distress trajectories across adulthood.

expressions.note: 适合把健康状态写成斜率和水平的共同变化。
expressions.example: Relationship types predict cognitive functioning trajectories.

expressions.note: 强调随年龄和阶段展开，而不是一次性横截面差异。
expressions.example: The gap widens across the life course.

expressions.note: 比 during marriage 更突出过程和阶段。
expressions.example: Occupational advantages evolve over the marital course.

expressions.note: domain 用于限定理论适用范围，避免过度泛化。
expressions.example: The hypothesis is weaker in the union domain.

expressions.en: The association may fade away as X ages.
expressions.note: 适合写 time-varying moderation，不等同于没有总体关联。
expressions.example: The moderation may fade away as women age.

expressions.en: Move from single transitions to whole trajectories.
expressions.note: 适合说明为什么 sequence/trajectory 视角比事件发生模型更合适。
expressions.example: We move from single transitions to whole trajectories.

expressions.en: Use cohort comparison to locate social change.
expressions.note: 适合缺少 period 识别时的谨慎解释。
expressions.example: Cohort comparisons locate changes in gender disparity.

clusters.meaning: 队列、差距扩大/缩小和社会变迁的动态表达

expressions.en: overlap (the overlap of X with Y)
expressions.note: overlap 强调事件同时发生或相互嵌套，不只是相关。
expressions.example: We examine the overlap of dissolution with parenthood.

expressions.note: 适合和 divergence、widening、narrowing 对照使用。
expressions.example: Convergence indicates that the occupational gap narrows.

expressions.note: 比 short duration 更自然，适合解释不稳定性后果。
expressions.example: The short-lived nature of cohabitation matters.

expressions.note: 比 changing relationship 更统计化，适合 growth curve。
expressions.example: We estimate time-varying associations across young adulthood.

expressions.note: serial 强调重复经历，不只是 one cohabitation。
expressions.example: Serial cohabitations have become more common.

expressions.note: 强调重复或持续暴露后的累积影响。
expressions.example: Multiple dissolutions may have cumulative consequences.

expressions.note: 比 among young adults 更动态，强调阶段跨度。
expressions.example: The association varies across young adulthood.

expressions.en: We examine how X varies over Y and across Z.
expressions.note: 适合 age/cohort 或 marital course/group 的双重比较。
expressions.example: We examine how depression varies over age and across cohorts.
